title: The burden of disease vs. GDP per capita
subtitle: The burden of disease is measured in Disability-Adjusted Life Years (DALYs). DALYs measure the total burden of disease – both from years of life lost due to premature death and years lived with a disability. One DALY equals one lost year of healthy life. Here it is age-standardized and expressed per 100,000 individuals.
caption:
The burden of disease vs. GDP per capita

The burden of disease is measured in Disability-Adjusted Life Years (DALYs). DALYs measure the total burden of disease – both from years of life lost due to premature death and years lived with a disability. One DALY equals one lost year of healthy life. Here it is age-standardized and expressed per 100,000 individuals.

This scatter plot displays the relationship between GDP per capita and the burden of disease for various countries in 2023, with GDP per capita measured in international-$ at 2021 prices to account for inflation and differences in living costs between countries. The horizontal axis uses a logarithmic scale ranging from $2,000 to $100,000, while the vertical axis measures DALYs per 100,000 people from 0 to 80,000. A clear negative correlation is visible across the dataset: countries with lower GDP per capita generally exhibit higher disease burdens. Nations in Africa, represented by purple markers, cluster in the upper-left quadrant; countries such as the Central African Republic, Niger, Chad, and Burundi show DALY values between 60,000 and 80,000 with GDP per capita below $2,000. In contrast, high-income nations in North America and Europe occupy the bottom-right section. The United States, Germany, and Japan appear with GDP per capita figures between $40,000 and $60,000 and DALY rates roughly between 15,000 and 30,000. Singapore and Greenland sit at the far right with GDP per capita exceeding $60,000 and DALY values near or below 20,000. Circle sizes indicate population, with large circles for India, China, Indonesia, and the United States showing their significant populations relative to smaller nations. Asian countries like India and China display intermediate positions, with DALYs ranging from approximately 20,000 to 40,000 and GDP per capita between $5,000 and $25,000.
